fault_hypothesis: The check on line 74 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), which incorrectly allows a new request when the number of active requests is exactly at the limit. It should use a greater-than-or-equal-to comparison (`>=`) to deny requests when the limit has been reached.

constraints.intent: The number of active requests should not be exactly equal to the limit at this decision point, as that case should be denied.
If the request is allowed (i.e., this branch is taken), the number of active requests must be strictly less than the limit.
The number of active timestamps cannot exceed the total number of timestamps provided.
